body:
Spend a little time with Governor Eric Holcomb, and you'll quickly discover that for him, his job is all about helping people. And that's why I... | | | [View this email in your browser](https://us11.campaign-archive.com/?e=1acd9bb3d6&u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=e494873f34)
---
| |
---
|
---
|
---
| **_Making Connections_** Spend a little time with **Governor Eric Holcomb** , and you'll quickly discover that for him, his job is all about helping people.

And that's why I wasn't surprised to learn that Next Level Connections, **Governor Holcomb's** 2019 infrastructure plan, is centered around Hoosiers, and helping make us all more connected to each other and the world. It tackles big issues facing our state head-on, delivering long worked-toward solutions. _And it delivers these solutions without raising taxes._

Check out the graphic above breaking down this $1 billion investment into Indiana infrastructure!

Just take rural broadband, for example. A recent Purdue University study tells us that about 93,000 Hoosiers are currently living in "internet darkness," meaning that they don't have access to affordable, high-speed broadband. In today's world, access to high-speed internet is critical for everything from farming to education to new advancements like tele-medicine. Without broadband access, these Hoosiers are wrongly disconnected from the world around them. It's the modern equivalent of not having a functional road to get home.

And that's why, as part of **Governor Holcomb's** Next Level Connections, Indiana will soon be dedicating $100 million to bring high-speed broadband infrastructure to these Hoosiers in need.

But it doesn't stop there. We all know that traveling to and from Southwest Indiana has gotten so much easier, thanks to the build-out of I-69 between Evansville and central Indiana. By investing $600 million to complete the final leg of I-69 -- finally providing a seamless connection between Evansville and Indianapolis, we'll be helping Hoosiers in this corner of our state gain critical access to the region around them.

**Governor Holcomb** is making a positive impact in the lives of all Hoosiers, and Next Level Connections is the latest big step! I can't wait to see what he next for Hoosiers!

-Kyle Hupfer
---
| |
---
| | **_Governor Holcomb's Week That Was_**
---
| [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=e43cfa0061&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| **_Next Level Connections_**
This week, **Governor Eric Holcomb** made stops throughout the state to share news about his 2019 infrastructure plan, Next Level Connections. As part of the plan, Indiana will be investing $1 billion into the infrastructure that connects us all -- broadband, roads, air, water, rail and trails.

[WATCH the video above NOW](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=23a7d1246f&e=1acd9bb3d6) from Tuesday's big event in Martinsville, and you'll hear Governor Holcomb detail it all himself...including:
* Making affordable, high-speed broadband available statewide
* Accelerating the completion of the final leg of I-69
* Improving U.S. 20 & U.S. 30, and reducing the stops on U.S. 31 between South Bend and Indianapolis
* Attracting more nonstop international flights to Indiana
* Building a fourth water port in Sotuhern Indiana
* Expanding rail service in Northwest Indiana
* Building more biking, hiking, and riding trails connecting communities statewide

But what's perhaps most exciting? Hoosiers will benefit from these big investments without paying higher taxes! Through a renegotiated contract with the Indiana Toll Road operator, heavy, mostly out-of-state trucks will pay higher tolls...but passenger vehicle tolls on this road will remain the same.

So...through Next Level Connections, Hoosiers are gaining better infrastructure...all through a program that is fully-funded! Make sure to [check out this video](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=7b5406bccd&e=1acd9bb3d6), and [share it](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=7b21f927ba&e=1acd9bb3d6) on social media!
---
|
---
| | **_The Mike Braun Corner_**
---
|
---
---
| **_On the Road with the Solutions Tour_**
This week, **Mike Braun** launched his Solutions Tour! He's traveling across Indiana to highlight solutions that he will work to deliver for Hoosiers as our next U.S. Senator. **Mike Braun’s** Solutions Tour this week included stops at Fleece Performance in Brownsburg, MasterSpas in Fort Wayne, and Green Leaf Inc. in Fontanet (near Terre Haute). He is talking with Hoosiers on the job about the real issues they face everyday, along with the lessons he has learned from running his own business in Southern Indiana.

Follow along with the Solutions Tour on Twitter at [@Braun4Indiana](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=33b7862544&e=1acd9bb3d6)!
---
|
---
|
---
| **_Democrats Attempt to Obstruct Kavanaugh Hearings_**
If you've been watching C-SPAN (or Twitter) this week, you may have noticed Democrats doing what they do best...attempting to obstruct real progress in our nation's capital.

This week, they did it as part of the confirmation hearings for Brett Kavanaugh, President Donald Trump's nominee for the U.S. Supreme Court. They brought in protesters and went on tirades against Judge Kavanaugh, a mainstream conservative judge who has built a track record of applying the law and U.S. Constitution as they're written.

The jury's still out, though, on whether Democrat Joe Donnelly -- one of Democrat Boss Chuck Schumer's cronies -- will be allowed to vote to confirm Judge Kavanaugh, or whether he'll continue to serve as a pawn in Democrats' political game.
---
|
---
|
---
| **_Join Us Next Weekend: National Day of Action!_** Next weekend, join Republicans across the country for the National Day of Action!

On September 15th, Hoosiers will be making calls, knocking on doors, and talking to their neighbors about Mike Braun and our entire 2018 statewide Republican team! Control of the United States Senate is in play, and Indiana is well positioned to defeat Joe Donnelly and protect our Republican majority! Volunteers from Lafayette to New Albany will be gathering all over the state, with one of the largest events being planned in Hamilton County. **\-- > Sign-up **[here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=0332994e4c&e=1acd9bb3d6) and we’ll get you plugged in to make a difference in your community next weekend!

But it doesn't stop there. We all know that traveling to and from Southwest Indiana has gotten so much easier, thanks to the build-out of I-69 between Evansville and central Indiana. By investing $600 million to complete the final leg of I-69 -- finally providing a seamless connection between Evansville and Indianapolis, we'll be helping Hoosiers in this corner of our state gain critical access to the region around them. Governor Holcomb is making a positive impact in the lives of all Hoosiers, and Next Level Connections is the latest big step! I can't wait to see what he next for Hoosiers! -Kyle Hupfer | | | | Governor Holcomb's Week That Was | | Next Level Connections This week, Governor Eric Holcomb made stops throughout the state to share news about his 2019 infrastructure plan, Next Level Connections. As part of the plan, Indiana will be investing $1 billion into the infrastructure that connects us all -- broadband, roads, air, water, rail and trails. from Tuesday's big event in Martinsville, and you'll hear Governor Holcomb detail it all himself...including: Making affordable, high-speed broadband available statewide Accelerating the completion of the final leg of I-69 Improving U.S. 20 & U.S. 30, and reducing the stops on U.S. 31 between South Bend and Indianapolis Attracting more nonstop international flights to Indiana Building a fourth water port in Sotuhern Indiana Expanding rail service in Northwest Indiana Building more biking, hiking, and riding trails connecting communities statewide But what's perhaps most exciting? Hoosiers will benefit from these big investments without paying higher taxes! Through a renegotiated contract with the Indiana Toll Road operator, heavy, mostly out-of-state trucks will pay higher tolls...but passenger vehicle tolls on this road will remain the same. So...through Next Level Connections, Hoosiers are gaining better infrastructure...all through a program that is fully-funded! Make sure to , and on social media! | | | The Mike Braun Corner | | On the Road with the Solutions Tour This week, Mike Braun launched his Solutions Tour! He's traveling across Indiana to highlight solutions that he will work to deliver for Hoosiers as our next U.S. Senator. Mike Braun’s Solutions Tour this week included stops at Fleece Performance in Brownsburg, MasterSpas in Fort Wayne, and Green Leaf Inc. in Fontanet (near Terre Haute). He is talking with Hoosiers on the job about the real issues they face everyday, along with the lessons he has learned from running his own business in Southern Indiana. Follow along with the Solutions Tour on Twitter at ! | | | Democrats Attempt to Obstruct Kavanaugh Hearings If you've been watching C-SPAN (or Twitter) this week, you may have noticed Democrats doing what they do best...attempting to obstruct real progress in our nation's capital. This week, they did it as part of the confirmation hearings for Brett Kavanaugh, President Donald Trump's nominee for the U.S. Supreme Court. They brought in protesters and went on tirades against Judge Kavanaugh, a mainstream conservative judge who has built a track record of applying the law and U.S. Constitution as they're written. The jury's still out, though, on whether Democrat Joe Donnelly -- one of Democrat Boss Chuck Schumer's cronies -- will be allowed to vote to confirm Judge Kavanaugh, or whether he'll continue to serve as a pawn in Democrats' political game. | | | Join Us Next Weekend: National Day of Action! Next weekend, join Republicans across the country for the National Day of Action! On September 15th, Hoosiers will be making calls, knocking on doors, and talking to their neighbors about Mike Braun and our entire 2018 statewide Republican team! Control of the United States Senate is in play, and Indiana is well positioned to defeat Joe Donnelly and protect our Republican majority! Volunteers from Lafayette to New Albany will be gathering all over the state, with one of the largest events being planned in Hamilton County. \-- > Sign-up and we’ll get you plugged in to make a difference in your community next weekend! | | Upcoming Republican Events NOTE: Due to heavy rain forecasted this weekend, tomorrow's 2018 Frye Picnic on the Farm in Greensburg has been cancelled. name: ZephyrForNY.com
subject: Our opponent called Zephyr "unhinged"
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Zephyr for NY
body:
This gross, misogynistic attack is unacceptable, and we cannot let it stand.
#

Peter -
At last night's final debate, one of our opponents took the personal and petty attacks to a new level. **He took a page out of the Republican playbook and used a gendered attack against Zephyr -- calling her "unhinged."**
Why? Because Zephyr correctly pointed out that he voted with Wall Street lobbyists to roll back key provisions of Dodd-Frank.
New York doesn't need an Attorney General who resorts to gendered attacks on a woman when he can't defend his record. Let's make sure that doesn't happen:
**[Will you make a $3 donation to send a message that we won't stand for these baseless attacks?](https://go.zephyrforny.com/page/m/4d78090c/6e822e2f/1d699c14/4173a7cc/2076727943/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0xODA5MDctMi1mdWxsIn0=/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/zephyr-september?refcode=em180907-2-full")**
It's clear that our momentum has our opponents scared, so they're getting desperate. But this gross, misogynistic attack is unacceptable, and we cannot let it stand.
Thanks for having Zephyr's back and pitching in to help us fight back.
\- Dan Torres
Campaign Manager
Zephyr for NY

name: Tim Kaine
subject: How I'll be voting on Judge Kavanaugh
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
\--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
| | \---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Derek,

This is important, and I wanted to share it with you: **I will oppose the nomination of Judge Brett Kavanaugh to the United States Supreme Court.**

I came to this decision after diligently studying his record of academic writings and judicial opinions, reading the limited documents we've been provided, interviewing him face-to-face in my office, and observing his testimony before the Senate Judiciary Committee. **It is my belief that Judge Kavanaugh should not be confirmed to the Supreme Court -- particularly at this pivotal time in our nation's history, because he cannot be counted on to serve as an independent check on executive power, or to uphold critical precedents that affect the well-being of millions of Americans.**
* * *
I'd like to tell you a little more about why I will be voting "no" on Judge Kavanaugh's confirmation. This is a long e-mail, but I hope you'll take the time to read my thoughts -- because this is a matter of profound importance to every single American.
* I. First, I have grave concerns about Judge Kavanaugh's commitment to serving as an independent check against unlawful action by the president. I asked Judge Kavanaugh about these matters when I interviewed him -- I was searching for something basic: _Would he have the backbone to stand against a president if the law compelled such a result?_ And I watched his testimony with the same question in mind. I found, at best, ambivalence -- not a firm commitment to independence.

* II. Second, Judge Kavanaugh's views about Supreme Court precedent do not inspire confidence. He has stated in his hearings that he views _Roe v. Wade_ as 'settled law' -- but in a 2003 email while working for the Bush Administration, he resisted characterizing Roe as 'settled law' since the 'Court can always overrule its precedent and three current Justices on the Court would do so.'

The number of justices on the Court who are hostile to reproductive rights has only grown since 2003. And given Judge Kavanaugh's professed admiration for Justice Rehnquist's dissenting opinion in Roe, **there is a significant possibility that he would join that group to deny women their right to make reproductive health care decisions.**

The candid admission that the 'Court can always overrule its precedent' raises a whole series of questions about what other 'settled' areas of law--in an era of 5-4 Supreme Court decisions--might suddenly be unsettled by this nominee. The constitutionality of the Affordable Care Act, the right of same-sex couples to marry, key immigration issues, the acceptability of reasonable rules to reduce gun violence, the ability to detain U.S. citizens as enemy combatants, and so many other issues currently under discussion could shift dramatically with this nominee.

**These matters are more than just hypothetical to families in Virginia. Judge Kavanaugh 's selective refusal to answer questions about issues deeply important to millions of Americans did nothing to allay these fears. **

When the Senate votes on Judge Kavanaugh's confirmation to the Supreme Court of the United States, I will vote "no."

Thank you for reading.

_Tim_ | [**DONATE**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f1123/3e0906/7222d05f/3e36285d/1460230473/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/tk-q32018-september-eoq?refcode=20180907_4_FR_TK_kavanaugh&amount=25&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=1+-+DONATE&utm_campaign=20180907_4_FR_TK_kavanaugh&source=20180907_4_FR_TK_kavanaugh")
---

* * *
|
---

name: Team Stanton
subject: beating Special Intersts
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stanton for Congress
body:
For too long, special interests have been able to buy off politicians by spending unlimited amounts of money through Super PACs.

That is happening in just about every race in America today, including in our own district. **[See what Greg has to say about it below:](https://act.myngp.com/el/vDjM3U18KDKla2jiejH7qh5XkOhMvVNI_pK--wfaeno=/UD-xEeI-244zmHdUDk78foqfyO5SwaNCovOy7NjjLnk=?refcode=X_090618_CLF_c&amount=25&recurring=auto)**

Paul Ryan's Super PAC is spending tens of thousands of dollars against Greg between now and November.

[**Can we count on you to help us overcome Paul Ryan 's Dark Money?**](https://act.myngp.com/el/vDjM3U18KDKla2jiejH7qh5XkOhMvVNI_pK--wfaeno=/KZZTWcBj9ESi-kZqm8Q4sSBgkTlfBm2lNnj-u94GbPA=?refcode=X_090618_CLF_c&amount=25&recurring=auto)

The only way to stop our special interests-backed opponent is to beat him on election day. We can't do it without your help.

Thank you!

\-- _Team Stanton _
